tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From 10 Strokes Down, Yoo Hae-ran Claims Her First Major in Historic Comeback
stories.summary: Yoo Hae-ran, 25, claimed her first major championship at the KPMG Women's PGA Championship, winning $1.95 million and ending a two-year drought for Korean players in majors.

stories.headline: Stranger Drives 200 Miles With a Truckful of Love for Cancer Family
stories.summary: Shawn Rizk drove 200 miles across Ontario with a pickup truck overflowing with community donations—furniture, gift cards, care packages—for the Reed family as Chris battles stage four cancer.

stories.headline: Canada's Eustaquio Sends Nation Into Euphoria with World Cup Breakthrough
stories.summary: Stephen Eustaquio's stoppage-time strike secured Canada's biggest men's soccer moment ever, lifting an entire nation into the World Cup knockout stage for the first time.

stories.headline: 175 AAA Employees Build 102 Bicycles for Maui's Foster Youth
stories.summary: One hundred seventy-five AAA employees built 102 bicycles as gifts for Maui's foster youth and children displaced by the wildfires.

stories.headline: Thai Qualifier Saves Match Point, Stuns Top-20 Seed at Wimbledon
stories.summary: Mananchaya Sawangkaew, 23, saved a match point and defeated 20th seed Maja Chwalinska in a stunning two-hour, 41-minute comeback, marking Thailand's first Grand Slam main draw victory in eight years.

stories.headline: Gainesville Family Serves Free Hot Meals to Neighbors Several Days a Week
stories.summary: Ashlee Graves and her family cook and serve free hot meals to neighbors several days a week, relying on donations and their own hearts to lift up their community.

stories.headline: Columbia Student Ranked 499th Stuns Cam Norrie at Wimbledon
stories.summary: Michael Zheng, ranked 499th just a year ago as a Columbia student, defeated Cameron Norrie in five sets at Wimbledon in his first-ever main draw match on grass.

stories.headline: More Than 60,000 Oysters Return to Scottish Waters After a Century
stories.summary: More than 60,000 European flat oysters returned to Scotland's Firth of Forth after a century of absence, doubling restoration targets and rebuilding ecosystems.

stories.headline: Volunteer Stitchers Create 5,000 Handmade Gifts Monthly for Those in Need
stories.summary: About 100 volunteers at Crafting for Communities stitch 5,000 handmade items monthly—blankets, toys, sensory kits—for people facing hardship across the West Midlands and beyond.

stories.headline: Injury-Ravaged New Zealand Achieves Rare Test Series Win in England
stories.summary: Injury-ravaged New Zealand achieved only their fourth test series victory in 95 years of touring England, lifting each other up through adversity.

stories.headline: Rare UK Butterfly Returns After Nearly 100 Years of Extinction
stories.summary: Dartmoor Zoo welcomed nine female black-veined white butterflies from Normandy, bringing back a species extinct in Britain since 1925, with one laying eggs within ten minutes of arrival.

stories.headline: Community Garden Opens Free Fresh Produce to All Who Need It
stories.summary: Trinity United Methodist Church and Carol Stoppkotte opened Haven Garden, a free community garden where anyone can harvest fresh beans, peppers, and tomatoes or help tend the soil.

stories.headline: Endangered Freshwater Pearl Mussels Return to Welsh River
stories.summary: Around 100 juvenile freshwater pearl mussels were released into a Welsh river tributary as part of an £8.8 million project to restore a species functionally extinct in many UK waterways.

stories.headline: Experimental Drug Shows Promise Against Huntington's Disease
stories.summary: The experimental drug anle138b significantly reduced toxic protein clumps in mouse brains and human stem cells, extending mobility and lifespan in Huntington's disease models.

stories.headline: Nanoparticles Could End the Need for Repeated Endometriosis Surgeries
stories.summary: Researchers developed gold-laced nanoparticles that hitchhike on white blood cells to identify and treat endometriosis without surgery, offering hope to millions of women in pain.

stories.headline: Table Sugar and Vinegar Could Make Vital Diabetes Drugs Cheaper and Faster
stories.summary: Scientists discovered a method using table sugar and vinegar to manufacture vital diabetes drugs, reducing 20 synthetic steps to just one, making treatment faster and cheaper.
